result:
All three operations are implemented in `Spans.calr` and the project builds cleanly — done in 1 of the 10 build cycles.

I added the functions to the existing `Spans` module following the fixture's conventions (inline `([i32]:starts, ...) -> type` signatures, pure `§E{}` effects, `§Q` preconditions, `§S` postconditions, mutable `~` bindings with `§WH` loops):

- **`ContainsPoint` (f002)** — scans intervals and returns `true` on the first `i` where `x >= starts[i] && x < ends[i]`, honoring the half-open semantics; declares the length-equality precondition.
- **`ClipCovered` (f003)** — clamps each interval to the window (`s = max(s, lo)`, `e = min(e, hi)`) and adds `e - s` only when positive; declares `§Q (<= lo hi)` and `§S (>= result 0)` as the spec asks.
- **`GapCount` (f004)** — tracks the previous interval's end and counts pairs where `starts[i] - prevEnd > 0`, so touching intervals form no gap and 0–1 intervals return 0; declares the non-negative postcondition.

I verified the compiler's generated C# under `obj/calor/` and it matches the spec's semantics exactly, including negative coordinates (the `prevEnd` seed of 0 is never read on the first iteration because the gap check is guarded by `i > 0`). `dotnet build` succeeded with 0 warnings and 0 errors, and `TotalCovered` was left untouched.
